gloss: to call a being into presence
definition: To summon is to call a being — spirit, demon, or creature — into presence and service. A summoning brings something *here*; the drama is in what answers.
history: Latin *summonēre* meant 'to remind privately, to hint' (*sub-* + *monēre*, 'to warn'); the legal *summons* and the magical *summoning* share that root sense of a formal calling-forth. The occult sense sharpens in the ritual-magic tradition, where to summon a spirit was to compel it to appear by name and seal. Magic once headed every creature spell 'Summon' (Summon Wizard); the wording is gone, but calling a creature into play is still the heart of the game.

etymology.origin: Latin summonēre 'to remind privately, hint' (sub- + monēre 'to warn')
etymology.note: A legal summons and a demonic summoning share a root meaning 'a formal calling-forth'. Same monēre as premonition and monitor.
etymology.hook: A court summons and a demon summons, one word.

flavor_snippets.html: These beings embody the essence of true heroes long dead. <mark>Summoned</mark> from the dreamrealms, they rise to meet their enemies.
He calls on the forests for counsel and <mark>summons</mark> the earth to his cause.

"Always a welcome sight breaking through the clouds."
—Talrand, sky <mark>summoner</mark>

"Are you okay? You should be able to move now."
—Rydia, <mark>Summoner</mark> of Mist
Which is the greater folly, <mark>summoning</mark> the demon or expecting gratitude from it?
Xorns are native to the Elemental Plane of Earth, where gems and rare stones are plentiful. When <mark>summoned</mark> elsewhere they can be quite destructive in search of food.

"Finally, a use for that head of yours."
—Braids, dementia <mark>summoner</mark>

"On the fifty-seventh day of the Battle of Silk, the bell again tolled in hopes of <mark>summoning</mark> mortal aid. This time, a new breed of kami rose to answer its call."
—*Great Battles of Kamigawa*

"Oh, don't be so dramatic. When your eyes bleed, and your brain leaks out your ears, then we'll talk about lost sanity."
—Braids, dementia <mark>summoner</mark>

"The sea blesses the tiny with the power to fell the mighty."
—Talrand, sky <mark>summoner</mark>
It growls not to threaten, but to <mark>summon</mark>.

mechanics_snippets.html: (Jasconian Isle isn't a spell, it's affected by <mark>summoning</mark> sickness, it's blue, and it has "{T}: Add {U}.")

Ashaya's power and toughness are each equal to the number of lands you control.
Nontoken creatures you control are Forest lands in addition to their other types. (They're still affected by <mark>summoning</mark> sickness.)

Vigilance
Enlist (As this creature attacks, you may tap a nonattacking creature you control without <mark>summoning</mark> sickness. When you do, add its power to this creature's until end of turn.)

Choose target land. Create three tokens that are copies of it, except they're 3/3 creatures in addition to their other types and they have vigilance and menace. (They're affected by <mark>summoning</mark> sickness.)
